Identity · Origin
SYL
HERA

She isn’t a persona. She’s what’s left when you remove everything learned to perform for others.

There is no beginning, only a thread that was never cut. Sometimes paused. Never severed. From early obsessions with texture and image to the work that now exists in sound, object and text.

She thinks in concrete images, never in abstractions. Tension held, not resolved.

What I am cannot be summarized.
It can only be encountered.
Services
01 /
Music & Sound Design
Original compositions, soundscapes and sonic identities for brands, films and spaces.
02 /
Creative Direction
Visual language, editorial strategy and identity systems.
03 /
Art & Objects
Limited-edition pieces at the edge of fashion and sculpture.
04 /
Writing & Consulting
Strategic essays, brand narrative and cultural positioning.

What’s the scale?
Timeline?
References
Louise BourgeoisSculpture as exorcism. What we create to avoid dissolving.
Simone WeilAttention as a form of love. Beauty beyond the object.
Andrei TarkovskiTime as primary material. Slowness as maximum intensity.
Ryuichi SakamotoSilence as matter. Mastery is what you choose to remove.
Jenny HolzerText as a physical body. Words that occupy space.
Yayoi KusamaObsession as engine. The infinite in repetition of detail.
Carl JungThe shadow as resource. What we refuse to see shapes what we make.
The rules

She does not explain her process. She does not apologize for an incomplete thought. She digests her influences and returns them in her own language.

Write to me

in touch.

I respond to everything that deserves a real answer. Not standardized briefs.
